span.p: GUI shape — one row per display

color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
position 1 → Group 1
position 2 → Group 1
position 3 → — None
position 4 → Group 2
'Group N' is a derived display label
computed from display order.
Identity is the opaque ID — see S2.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Conversion is one-way at runtime.
JSON is canonical, engine is derived.
list-of-lists never mutated by hand.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Single-group constraint is structural.
groupOf is a map keyed by position.
A position has ONE group ID.
Cannot represent overlap by construction.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Why opaque group IDs?
Letters and numbers reshuffle on changes.
When groups gain metadata in v2
(custom name, color, per-group switchDelay),
label-keyed metadata would follow the wrong
group. Opaque IDs survive every reshuffle.
Group N labels are render-time only.

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Why is spaceNames in the SAME JSON
keyed by group ID, not split into hs.settings?
Earlier draft kept names in hs.settings keyed
by sorted-position-tuple ('1,2,3'). Reshuffling
groupOf silently stranded names under stale
keys. Group-ID-keyed names in JSON solve this
structurally - reshuffles don't change group IDs,
so names follow membership automatically.
The 'workspace' noun was rejected: names are
just Space names that happen to apply per-group.
No new term needed; the cross-display behavior
is a property of being in a sync group.

svg.text: SpacesSync data model (revised after vocab council)
